diversity_basis.citation: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Reg 552 (motor diversity, by analogy) + IET OSG App A motor section + industry practice — 100% largest + 75% remainder for multi-split AC groups; no single CIBSE pinpoint clause verified, engineer-of-record must validate against project load profile. 4 × 3.5 kW: 3.5 + 0.75×10.5 = 11.375 kW → factor = 11.375/14 = 0.81

diversity_basis.citation: IET OSG App A — socket-outlets: 100% largest + 40% remainder. 6 × 1.0 kW = 6.0 kW total; largest 1.0 kW: 1.0 + 0.4×5.0 = 3.0 kW → factor = 3.0/6 = 0.50.

assumptions: Diversity computed per IET OSG App A + BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 311.1 (NOT a blanket factor). Per-load-type diversity_basis is surfaced on every circuit. 6 distinct load types covered: lift (EN 81-20 §5.10 + BS 7671 Section 552, 1.00), EV (Reg 722 + IET CoP for EVCI 4th Ed §8.5, 1.00), AC single (Section 552 by analogy, 1.00), AC group (engineer-declared 100+75 by analogy to Reg 552 → 0.81), socket_general (OSG App A 100+40 → 0.50), lighting_continuous (OSG App A, 1.00).

IET CoP for EVCI is industry guidance (BS 7671 Reg 722 statutory references industry compliance against it); AC group 100+75 rule is engineer-declared industry practice with no single authoritative pinpoint clause — both flagged inline on each citation per [[feedback-no-trim-non-consequential]].
Cable ampacity check assumes BS 7671 Appendix 4 Reference Method C (clipped direct).

rationale.chat_summary: UK mixed-use SDB feeding 1 lift + 4 EV chargers + AC (single + grouped 4-unit multi-split) + lighting + general sockets. Per-circuit diversity_basis populated for all 9 circuits demonstrating 6 distinct load types: lift (EN 81-20 §5.10 + BS 7671 Section 552, 1.00), EV (Reg 722 + IET CoP for EVCI 4th Ed §8.5, 1.00), AC single (Section 552 by analogy, 1.00), AC group (engineer-declared 100+75 by analogy → 0.81), sockets (OSG App A 100+40 → 0.50), lighting (OSG App A, 1.00). Total demand = 61.815 kW = 99.2 A on 200 A TPN main (~50% headroom).

sections.title: Why these load types
sections.summary: Example chosen to demonstrate all 4 new diversity rows added in Sprint D2.3 (lifts + EV + AC single + AC group) plus tightened motor + socket citations. Mixed-use ground-floor common-area board is realistic UK scenario where all 5 load types coexist.

sections.title: Lift diversity (EN 81-20 + BS 7671 Section 552)
sections.summary: C01 lift: factor 1.00. EN 81-20:2020 §5.10 (lift electrical installation) + BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Section 552 (motor circuits). BS 7671 has no dedicated lift regulation; the safety envelope comes from EN 81-20 and the motor-circuit aspect from Section 552. Lift motors take full demand because starting transients (3-5× running current) plus emergency/fire-mode duty cycle preclude diversity. Type B RCD required for VFD-driven lift motors per BS 7671 Reg 531.3.3 (RCD type selection when DC residual current components are present).

sections.title: EV diversity (Reg 722 + IET CoP for EVCI)
sections.summary: C02-C05 EV: factor 1.00 each. BS 7671 Reg 722 (statutory) is the legal basis; IET Code of Practice for EV Charging Equipment Installation (4th Ed) §8.5 is the industry-standard reference for Reg 722 compliance. EV charging is treated as continuous load at full demand — no diversity. Type B RCD required per Reg 722.531.3.101 (DC fault current detection).

sections.title: AC diversity (single vs multi-split group)
sections.summary: C06 AC single: factor 1.00 (no diversity — single compressor at full demand; engineer-declared by analogy to BS 7671 Section 552 motor-circuit rules). C07 AC multi-split group of 4 × 3.5 kW: factor 0.81 via engineer-declared 100% largest + 75% remainder, by analogy to BS 7671 Reg 552 motor diversity + IET OSG App A motor section + industry practice. No single authoritative pinpoint clause for multi-split AC diversity exists in published UK guidance — engineer-of-record must validate against project-specific load profile.

sections.title: IET CoP for EVCI industry-guidance status (honest disclosure)
sections.summary: The IET Code of Practice for EV Charging Equipment Installation (4th Ed, 2020; updated 5th Ed, 2023) is INDUSTRY GUIDANCE, not statutory law. However, BS 7671 Reg 722 IS statutory and the IET CoP is the industry-standard reference for Reg 722 compliance. Both citations appear in every EV circuit's diversity_basis.citation field per the [[feedback-no-trim-non-consequential]] honest-disclosure rule.

sections.title: AC group diversity uncertainty (honest disclosure)
sections.summary: The 100% largest + 75% remainder rule for multi-split AC groups is engineering practice without a single authoritative pinpoint clause in published UK guidance. The closest published anchor is BS 7671 Section 552 motor diversity applied by analogy. The C07 citation flags this inline; engineer-of-record must validate against project-specific load profile. INV-15 Rule 2 (citation contains a recognisable clause marker) is still satisfied via the Reg / § / OSG markers in the citation string.

sections.title: Board-level demand
sections.summary: Sum of per-circuit demands: lift 11.0 + EV 4 × 7.36 + AC single 5.0 + AC group 11.375 + lighting 2.0 + sockets 3.0 = 61.815 kW. At 400 V TPN 0.9 pf: I = 99.2 A balanced on 200 A main — 50% headroom. Per-phase loading reasonably balanced (EV phases rotated by design).

invariants.evidence: Diversity factor for shower-equivalent loads on this board: N/A (no instantaneous loads present). For the loads that ARE present, per-load-type factors applied per generator step; no blanket factor used. INV-12 Sprint B M1 rule (instantaneous loads must use 1.00) not triggered because no instantaneous-load circuits exist on this SDB.

label_format_standard=BS matches jurisdiction=GB (Rule 1 PASS). board_label.text 'SDB-COMMON | 400 V TPN | Main Switch 200 A | —' matches BS regex (Rule 2-3 PASS). All 9 circuit_label entries populated with text + svg per BS template; no {{placeholders}} remain in any svg (Rules 4-5 PASS). tool_call_pending_for_pdf_png=true on board_label + all 9 circuits (Rule 6 PASS).

INV-15 board SDB-COMMON (GB): all 9 circuits carry diversity_basis. load_types: lift (×1), ev_charger (×4), ac_single (×1), ac_group (×1), lighting_continuous (×1), socket_general (×1) — all valid enum (Rule 1 PASS). Citations all ≥20 chars with recognisable clause markers (Reg / § / OSG / CoP — citations point to BS 7671 + EN 81-20 + IET CoP for EVCI 4th Ed + IET OSG App A) (Rule 2 PASS). Lift factor=1.00 method=no_diversity per EN 81-20 §5.10 + BS 7671 Section 552; 4 EV factor=1.00 method=no_diversity per Reg 722 + IET CoP EVCI §8.5 (Rule 3 PASS). C07 AC group method_params {100,75} sums 175 ∈ [100,200]; C09 sockets {100,40} sums 140 ∈ [100,200] (Rule 4 PASS).
